The Senate has passed a reconciliation bill using a 'current policy' baseline, which extends the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts. President Trump signed the bill on July 4, 2025. The bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), makes the expiring rate and bracket changes and other individual provisions of the 2017 TCJA permanent.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. This law introduces significant changes to the clean energy tax credit landscape established by the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). Specifically, the OBBBA harmonizes and enhances domestic content requirements under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025, making domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y).

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48E) have been made mandatory. President Trump signed H.R.1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on July 4, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits and imposes specific domestic content percentages for Section 48E projects. These requirements are now mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that would require domestic materials or subcomponents for certain tax credits, taking effect under specific conditions.
